text: "Of David," as we read in Hebrew and the Alexandrian Septuagint, being composed by him, when all Israel acknowledged his dominion, (Berthier) after his third anointing. (Houbigant)<hr>Others explain it of the captives returned, and particularly of the sacred ministers resolving to perform their duties with unanimity. (Calmet)<hr>Esdras might propose the example "of David," for the imitation of others. (Worthington)
